title: quassia glabra
intro: Quassia glabra, also known as the lapacho or quassia, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic agent. The health benefits of Quassia glabra include its potential to treat a variety of ailments, such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce the risk of certain diseases. Quassia glabra can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
